## Answers to common questions about matching, scale, and data.

### How does the platform handle users who act as both a buyer and a seller?+

In a marketplace, a single human often wears two hats. Iterable manages this through a Unified User Profile that uses a single unique identifier (like an email or UserID) while supporting custom data schemas.

### Is it possible to orchestrate journeys across both buyer and seller channels in a single workflow?+

Yes. Iterable’s Studio (journey builder) allows for cross-audience orchestration. You can create a tansaction loop workflow where a buyer action (like “item purchased”) triggers a simultaneous branch for the seller (“ship your item”).

### How does Iterable manage high-frequency data spikes common in marketplace transaction cycles?+

Marketplaces experience massive volatility (e.g., Black Friday or sudden viral product drops). Iterable is built on an elasticsearch foundation designed for scalability.

*   Real-Time Ingestion: Our API can ingest millions of custom events per second without lagging your messaging.

*   Frequency Optimization: To prevent “notification fatigue” during high-activity periods, Iterable AI automatically determines the ideal message cadence for each user, ensuring you don’t burn out your audience during a peak cycle.
*   Intelligent triggers: Infrastructure optimized for flash sales ensures that time-sensitive alerts land in inboxes or on screens within seconds of the trigger.

### How does predictive AI assist in preventing marketplace “leakage” or transaction churn?+

“Leakage”—when users move their transaction off-platform to avoid fees—is a marketplace killer. Iterable uses Predictive Goals and AI Agents to intervene before the churn happens.

Connect buyers and sellers faster and more reliably with Iterable.
